title: nauclea cinchonae
intro: Nauclea cinchonae, also known as Quinine tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is a tree native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties. The bark of the Nauclea cinchonae tree contains quinine, a compound that has been used to treat malaria and other fevers due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Nauclea cinchonae include reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of malaria, and providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be prepared through various herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions.
